html:
[848]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 顷之 - Temporal expression meaning 'after a while' or 'shortly after.'
  • 具以曲折告 - Means 'to tell in detail' or 'to recount fully,' where '曲折' refers to the complicated details.
  • 干净室设榻 - Means 'to prepare a clean room and set up a bed,' a ritualistic preparation for a spiritual or magical event.
  • 明日复来 - Means 'to return the next day,' indicating a planned follow-up.
  • 须正午乃启门 - Means 'to open the door only at noon,' a specific timing for the event.
  • 寒气逼人 - Means 'the cold air is chilling,' describing a physical sensation.
  • 刀剑戛击之声不绝 - Means 'the sound of swords clashing does not cease,' a metaphor for conflict or tension.
  • 一髑髅，如五斗大 - Means 'a skull, as large as five dou,' a vivid and unusual description of the object.
  • 出箧中药一刀圭糁之，悉化为水 - Means 'to take out a small amount of medicine from a box, sprinkle it on the skull, and it all turns to water,' a magical or alchemical process.
  • 吾与女子皆剑仙 - Means 'I and the woman are both sword immortals,' indicating their supernatural status.
  • 女先与一人绸缪，遽舍而从汝，以故怀忿，欲杀汝二人 - Means 'the woman had a previous intimate relationship with someone, then left him to follow you, which is why she harbors resentment and wants to kill both of you,' a complex narrative of betrayal and revenge.
  • 吾亦相与有宿契，特出力救汝，今事幸获济，吾亦去矣 - Means 'I also have a predestined agreement with you, so I have come to your aid, and now that the matter is resolved, I will leave,' explaining the intervention and departure.
  • 才去，女即来 - Means 'as soon as he left, the woman arrived,' indicating a quick transition.
  • 遂同室如初 - Means 'they then lived together as before,' suggesting a return to normalcy.
  • 罹姜母之丧，哀器呕血 - Means 'they mourned the death of Jia's mother, and she wept so much that she vomited blood,' a sign of deep grief.
  • 姜妻继亡，抚育其子如己出 - Means 'Jia's wife also died, and he raised her son as his own,' showing continued care and responsibility.
  • 靖康之变，不知所终 - Means 'during the Jingkang Incident, their fate is unknown,' referring to a significant historical event.
  • 廉夫后寓鄱阳而卒 - Means 'Lianfu later lived in Poyang and died,' providing a brief biography.
  • 厥孙曰好古，至今为饶人 - Means 'his grandson is named Hao Gu, and he is still alive today,' indicating the continuation of the family line.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 剑仙 - Refers to a type of immortal who practices swordsmanship, a common theme in Chinese mythology and folklore.
  • 靖康之变 - Refers to the Jingkang Incident, a significant event in Chinese history where the Jin Dynasty captured the Song Dynasty capital, leading to the fall of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 鄱阳 - Refers to Poyang, a city in Jiangxi Province, China, where Lianfu lived out his later years.
  • 饶人 - Refers to a person who is still alive, indicating the continuity of the family lineage.

Proper Nouns

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner.
  • 姜 - Refers to Jia, the main character in the story.
  • 髑髅 - Refers to a skull, a significant object in the story.
  • 剑仙 - Refers to a type of immortal who practices swordsmanship.
  • 靖康之变 - Refers to the Jingkang Incident, a significant event in Chinese history.
  • 鄱阳 - Refers to Poyang, a city in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 廉夫 - Refers to Lianfu, a character in the story.
  • 好古 - Refers to Hao Gu, the grandson of Lianfu.

[849]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艳异编卷二十五祖异部 - Title of the text, translating to 'Volume 25 of *Yan Yi Bian*: Ancestral Anomalies Department'. *Yan Yi Bian* is a collection of strange and unusual stories.
  • 却要 - Name of the maid, a proper noun that should remain unchanged.
  • 朔望 - Refers to the first day (朔) and the fifteenth day (望) of the lunar month, when rituals and visits are traditionally made.
  • 通礼谒于亲姻家 - Means 'to perform the ritual of visiting relatives and in-laws', a traditional practice during these days.
  • 巧媚才捷 - Describes the maid as 'graceful, charming, and quick-witted'.
  • 承顺颜色 - Means 'to follow the moods and wishes of others'.
  • 四子 - Refers to the four sons of Li Geng.
  • 大郎而下五郎也 - A colloquial way of listing the sons, meaning 'from the eldest son (大郎) down to the fifth son'.
  • 清明节 - Refers to the Qingming Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday for honoring ancestors.
  • 纤月娟娟 - Describes a thin, beautiful crescent moon.
  • 庭花烂发 - Means 'the garden flowers are in full bloom'.
  • 垂绣幕，张银 - Describes the hanging embroidered curtains and the spread of silver (possibly lanterns or decorations).
  • 樱桃花影中 - Refers to the shadow of cherry blossoms.
  • 欲持之求偶 - Means 'wanted to take her for a companion'.
  • 取茵席授之 - Means 'took a mat and gave it to him'.
  • 伫立相待 - Means 'to wait standing still'.
  • 眠熟 - Means 'to sleep soundly'.
  • 握手不可解 - Means 'held hands and could not let go'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖南观察使 - Refers to the governor of Hunan, a position in the Tang Dynasty administrative system.
  • 李庚 - The name of the governor, a proper noun.
  • 清明节 - The Qingming Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday for honoring ancestors and spring outings.
  • 樱桃花影中 - Cherry blossoms are a symbol of beauty and transience in Chinese culture, often associated with spring and fleeting moments.

Proper Nouns

  • 湖南观察使 - The position of governor of Hunan.
  • 李庚 - The name of the governor.
  • 却要 - The name of the maid.
  • 清明节 - The Qingming Festival.

[850]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 延禧于厅角中 - Describes the action of hiding in a corner of the hall.
  • 屏息以待 - Means to hold one's breath while waiting, indicating a state of anticipation or anxiety.
  • 比比而至 - Means 'arriving one after another,' suggesting a continuous arrival.
  • 各趋一隅 - Means 'each moving to a corner,' indicating that the brothers are spreading out.
  • 心虽讶之，而不敢发 - Means 'although surprised, they dared not speak out,' indicating a sense of fear or restraint.
  • 却要突燃烛 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous but likely means 'suddenly lighting a candle.'
  • 豁开扉而照之 - Means 'opening the door and illuminating it,' indicating the act of lighting a candle and opening the door.
  • 阿堵贫儿，争敢向这里觅宿处 - This is a colloquial expression, where '阿堵' is a colloquial term for 'this' or 'that,' and '贫儿' means 'poor child.' The phrase is a harsh rebuke, meaning 'How dare this poor child look for a place to stay here!'
  • 皆弃所携，掩面而走 - Means 'all abandoned what they were carrying and fled with their faces covered,' indicating a hurried and ashamed departure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间 - A place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 戚里 - Refers to the noble quarters or the residence of the nobility.
  • 车缕 - Refers to a carriage or vehicle, often used in classical texts to symbolize wealth and status.

Proper Nouns

  • 延禧 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 河间 - A place name, likely referring to a region in China.
  • 戚里 - A place name, likely referring to the noble quarters or the residence of the nobility.

[851]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 姑怒曰 - Mother-in-law angrily said, introducing direct speech.
  • 以一接新妇，求为得师 - To meet the new bride for the first time, seeking to become her teacher, a specific request.
  • 闻妇之道，以贞顺静专为 - To learn the way of a woman, which is to be chaste, obedient, calm, and focused, describing traditional virtues.
  • 若夫矜车服、耀首饰，族出灌门，以饮食游观，非妇人宜也 - Such as showing off carriages and clothing, dazzling with jewelry, coming from noble families, and indulging in feasts and sightseeing, are not suitable for women, listing inappropriate behaviors.
  • 姑强之，乃从之游 - The mother-in-law forced her, so she went with her, indicating coercion.
  • 过市，或曰 - Passing through the market, someone said, introducing a suggestion.
  • 市少南人浮图，有国工吴叟始图东南壁甚怪 - The market has a small temple built by a southern person, with a national craftsman named Wu who started painting the southeast wall in a very strange manner, describing a specific location and artist.
  • 可使奚官先避道，乃人观 - You can make the official go first to clear the way, then you can enter and see, giving instructions.
  • 观已，延及客佐具食 - After viewing, invite guests to prepare food, describing a sequence of actions.
  • 帏床之侧闻男子咳者 - Hearing a man coughing near the curtained bed, describing an unexpected sound.
  • 河间惊，跣足出，召从者驰车归 - The woman named Hejian was startled, took off her shoes, and called her attendants to ride in a carriage back home, describing her reaction.
  • 泣数日，愈自闭，不与众戚通 - She cried for several days, became more isolated, and no longer communicated with the other relatives, describing her emotional state.
  • 戚里乃更来谢曰 - The relatives then came to apologize, saying, introducing an apology.
  • 河间之遽也，犹以前故，得无罪吾属也 - The suddenness of Hejian's reaction, still due to the previous reason, could it be that we are at fault? Expressing concern.
  • 向之咳者，为膳奴耳 - The one who coughed earlier was just a kitchen servant, clarifying the identity of the person.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姑 - Refers to the mother-in-law.
  • 新妇 - Refers to the newly married woman.
  • 贞顺静专 - Traditional virtues of a woman, emphasizing chastity, obedience, calmness, and focus.
  • 矜车服、耀首饰 - Describes showing off luxurious carriages and jewelry, indicating a display of wealth.
  • 族出灌门 - Indicates coming from a noble family, specifically the Guan clan.
  • 饮食游观 - Refers to indulging in feasts and sightseeing, indicating a luxurious lifestyle.
  • 奚官 - Refers to an official or attendant.
  • 帏床 - Refers to a curtained bed, indicating a private space.
  • 河间 - The name of the woman, possibly referring to a place or a person.
  • 戚里 - Refers to relatives or close family members.
  • 膳奴 - Refers to a kitchen servant.

Proper Nouns

  • 姑 - The mother-in-law.
  • 新妇 - The newly married woman.
  • 河间 - The name of the woman.
  • 戚里 - The relatives or close family members.
  • 膳奴 - The kitchen servant.
  • 奚官 - The official or attendant.
  • 吴叟 - The national craftsman.

[852]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 期年 - A full year, indicating a significant period of time.
  • 乃敢复召 - "乃敢" means "only dare" or "finally dared," suggesting a reluctance or hesitation.
  • 邀于姑 - "邀" means "invite," and "于姑" likely refers to an aunt or a respected elder female figure.
  • 必致之与偕行 - "必致之" means "must bring," and "偕行" means "together with," indicating a strong insistence on bringing someone along.
  • 遂人礼州西浮图 - "遂人礼" means "followed the ritual," and "州西浮图" refers to a pagoda or tower in the west of the state.
  • 两阁叩槛出鱼艳食之 - "两阁" means "two pavilions," "叩槛" means "knock on the railing," and "出鱼艳食之" suggests serving a dish called "鱼艳" (a type of fish dish).
  • 河间为一笑 - "河间" is a place name, and "为一笑" means "smiled," indicating a reaction from the people of Hejian.
  • 众乃欢 - "众" means "the crowd," and "乃欢" means "then rejoiced."
  • 俄而又引至食所 - "俄而" means "soon after," and "引至食所" means "led to the dining area."
  • 空无帷幕 - "空无" means "empty," and "帷幕" means "curtains," indicating the absence of decorations.
  • 廊庑廓然 - "廊庑" means "hallways and corridors," and "廓然" means "spacious and open."
  • 河间乃肯入 - "河间" is a place name, and "乃肯入" means "finally agreed to enter."
  • 先壁群恶少于北牖下 - "先壁" means "previously hidden," "群恶少" means "a group of bad young men," and "北牖下" means "under the north window."
  • 降帘 - "降帘" means "lower the curtains."
  • 使女子为秦声 - "使" means "make," "女子" means "women," and "为秦声" means "perform Qin music."
  • 倨坐观之 - "倨坐" means "sit arrogantly," and "观之" means "watch it."
  • 有顷 - "有顷" means "after a while."
  • 壁者出 - "壁者" means "the hidden ones," and "出" means "came out."
  • 宿选貌美阴大者主河间 - "宿选" means "previously selected," "貌美阴大者" means "beautiful and large-breasted," and "主河间" means "take charge of Hejian."
  • 乃便抱持河间 - "乃便" means "then immediately," "抱持" means "hold tightly," and "河间" is a place name.
  • 河间号且泣 - "河间" is a place name, "号" means "cry out," and "且泣" means "and weep."
  • 婢夹持之 - "婢" means "maidservants," and "夹持之" means "hold her tightly from both sides."
  • 或谕以利 - "或" means "some," "谕" means "explain," and "以利" means "with benefits."
  • 或骂且笑之 - "或" means "some," "骂" means "scold," and "笑之" means "laugh at her."
  • 河间窃顾视 - "河间" is a place name, "窃" means "secretly," and "顾视" means "look around."
  • 持己者甚美 - "持己者" means "the person holding her," and "甚美" means "very beautiful."
  • 左右为不善者 - "左右" means "those around," and "为不善者" means "who do bad things."
  • 已更得适意 - "已" means "already," "更得" means "gained," and "适意" means "pleasure."
  • 鼻息然 - "鼻息然" means "sniffing contentedly."
  • 意不能无动 - "意" means "mind," "不能无动" means "cannot remain unmoved."
  • 力稍纵 - "力" means "strength," and "稍纵" means "slightly relaxed."
  • 主者幸一遂焉 - "主者" means "the one in charge," "幸" means "fortunately," and "一遂焉" means "achieved it once."
  • 因拥致之房 - "因" means "then," "拥" means "carry," "致之" means "bring her to," and "房" means "room."
  • 河间收泣甚适 - "河间" is a place name, "收泣" means "stop crying," and "甚适" means "very comfortable."
  • 自庆未始得也 - "自庆" means "congratulate oneself," "未始得" means "never had before."
  • 至日仄食 - "至" means "until," "日仄" means "midday," and "食" means "meal."
  • 其类呼之食 - "其类" means "her kind," "呼之食" means "call her to eat."
  • 吾不食矣 - "吾" means "I," "不食矣" means "will not eat anymore."
  • 且暮 - "且" means "about to," and "暮" means "evening."
  • 驾车相戒归 - "驾车" means "drive the carriage," "相戒" means "mutually warn," and "归" means "return."
  • 群戚反大闷 - "群戚" means "the crowd," "反" means "turned," and "大闷" means "greatly depressed."
  • 不得已俱宿焉 - "不得已" means "unwillingly," "俱宿焉" means "stay together."
  • 夫骑来迎，莫得见 - "夫骑" means "the husband's horsemen," "来迎" means "come to fetch," and "莫得见" means "could not see."
  • 左右力制，明日乃肯归 - "左右" means "those around," "力制" means "forcefully control," "明日" means "the next day," and "乃肯归" means "finally agreed to return."
  • 持淫夫大泣 - "持" means "hold," "淫夫" means "the immoral man," and "大泣" means "weep greatly."
  • 啮臂相与盟 - "啮臂" means "bite their arms," and "相与盟" means "make an oath together."
  • 而后就车 - "而后" means "then," "就车" means "get into the carriage."
  • 既归，不忍视其夫，闭目曰：吾病。 - "既归" means "after returning," "不忍视其夫" means "could not bear to look at her husband," "闭目曰" means "closed her eyes and said," and "吾病" means "I am ill."

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
  • 浮图 - Refers to a pagoda or tower, often associated with Buddhist temples.
  • 秦声 - Refers to music from the Qin state, known for its distinctive style.
  • 北牖 - Refers to the north window, often associated with a place of seclusion or privacy.
  • 阴大者 - Refers to a woman with a large chest, often associated with beauty in classical Chinese literature.
  • 主河间 - Likely refers to a high-ranking official or noblewoman in charge of the region of Hejian.
  • 鼻息然 - A metaphor for contentment or satisfaction.
  • 左右为不善者 - Refers to people who engage in immoral or unethical behavior.
  • 啮臂相与盟 - A ritual of making a pact by biting each other's arms, symbolizing a strong bond.

[853]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 与之百物，卒不食 - Gave her a hundred things, but she still would not eat. Indicates persistence in offering food without success.
  • 饵以善药，挥去 - Fed her good medicine, but she waved it away. Indicates rejection of even beneficial remedies.
  • 心怦怦恒若危柱之弦 - Her heart beat like a string on a precarious pillar. Vivid metaphor for extreme anxiety or fear.
  • 夫耒辄大骂，终不一开目 - Her husband, whenever he tried to approach, would curse her, and she would not even open her eyes once. Describes a persistent state of hostility or indifference.
  • 愈益恶之，夫不胜其忧 - She grew increasingly hateful, and her husband could not bear his worries. Conveys deepening resentment and distress.
  • 数日，乃曰 - After several days, she said... Sets up a delayed response or revelation.
  • 吾病且死，非药饵能已 - I am dying of my illness, and no medicine can cure it. Direct statement of despair.
  • 为吾召鬼解除之，然必以夜 - Summon a ghost to rid me of this, but only at night. Specific and ominous request.
  • 其夫自河间病，言如狂人 - Her husband, who had been ill in Hejian, spoke like a madman. Suggests a mental or emotional breakdown.
  • 度无不为 - He considered doing anything. Implies extreme desperation.
  • 时上恶夜祠 - At that time, the emperor disliked night rituals. Historical context crucial for understanding legal and social implications.
  • 既张具，河间命邑臣，告其夫召鬼祝诅上 - After setting up the ritual, the officials from Hejian reported that she had summoned a ghost to curse the emperor. Specific accusation with serious consequences.
  • 下吏讯验，笞杀之 - The officials interrogated and verified, then beat her to death. Describes a brutal and swift punishment.
  • 将死犹曰：“吾负夫人，吾负夫人。” - As she was dying, she said, 'I have wronged my wife, I have wronged my wife.' Final statement adds a layer of moral complexity.
  • 河间大喜，不为服，开门召所与淫者 - The officials from Hejian were very pleased, did not mourn for her, and opened the door to call in those she had been intimate with. Shows a lack of respect and a focus on personal gratification.
  • 倮逐为荒淫 - They chased them naked and engaged in debauchery. Describes a scene of moral degradation.
  • 居一岁，所淫者衰，益厌，乃出之 - After a year, those she had been intimate with declined, and she grew even more disgusted, so she sent them away. Shows a shift in her behavior.
  • 召长安无赖男子，晨夜交于门 - She called in worthless men from Chang'an, engaging in intercourse day and night at the door. Continues the theme of moral decay.
  • 犹不慊 - Still, she was not satisfied. Indicates ongoing dissatisfaction.
  • 又为酒垆西南隅，己居楼上微观之 - She also set up a wine shop in the southwest corner, living upstairs and watching them. Shows her continued involvement in this lifestyle.
  • 凿小门，以女侍饵焉 - She dug a small door, and had female attendants lure them in. Describes a calculated and manipulative approach.
  • 凡来饮酒大鼻者，少且壮者，美颜色者，善为戏酒者，皆上与合，且合且窥 - Anyone who came to drink, with a big nose, young and strong, beautiful appearance, or skilled at playing with wine, would all be with her, and she would watch them. Lists the types of men she found attractive and her method of attraction.
  • 恐失一男子也，犹日呻呼懵懵，以为不足 - She feared losing even one man, and still moaned and groaned every day, thinking it was not enough. Conveys her obsessive and insatiable nature.
  • 积十余年，病髓竭而死 - After more than ten years, she died from exhaustion of her marrow. Describes a prolonged and ultimately fatal condition.
  • 自是虽戚里为邪行者，闻河间之名，则掩鼻蹙额，皆不欲道也 - From then on, even those in the nobility who engaged in immoral behavior, when they heard of the name of Hejian, would cover their noses and wrinkle their brows, and did not want to mention it. Shows the lasting negative impact of her actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间 - Refers to the region of Hejian, known for its historical significance and the presence of the officials involved.
  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital city during the Han dynasty, known for its cultural and social life.
  • 无赖男子 - Refers to worthless or disreputable men, often associated with lower-class or morally questionable individuals.
  • 酒垆 - A wine shop or tavern, a common social gathering place in ancient China.
  • 祝诅 - Refers to the act of cursing or invoking evil spirits, a serious offense in ancient Chinese law.
  • 笞杀 - Refers to the practice of beating to death, a form of punishment in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 河间 - Region of Hejian, known for its historical significance and the presence of the officials involved.
  • 长安 - Capital city during the Han dynasty, known for its cultural and social life.

[854]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天下之士为修洁者 - Refers to the men of the world who are virtuous and clean in character.
  • 河间之始为妻妇者乎 - Refers to the woman from the River Region who is newly married.
  • 朋友相慕望 - Means mutual admiration and longing among friends.
  • 切密 - Means close and intimate.
  • 强暴 - Refers to violent and powerful people or forces.
  • 诚服其利 - Means to truly submit to their advantage.
  • 归敌其夫 - Means to turn against her husband.
  • 犹盗贼仇雠 - Means like thieves and enemies.
  • 不忍一视其面 - Means unable to look at her face.
  • 卒计以杀之 - Means eventually decided to kill him.
  • 无须臾之戚 - Means without any moment of sorrow.
  • 邪利之猾其中 - Means evil gains that are deceitful within.
  • 恩之难恃 - Means the difficulty of relying on kindness.
  • 君臣之际 - Refers to the relationship between ruler and minister.
  • 尤可畏哉 - Means especially to be feared.
  • 予故私自列云 - Means I therefore privately write this.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间 - Refers to the River Region, likely a specific area known for its beauty or significance.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city, likely the imperial capital during the time period.
  • 省试 - Refers to the provincial examination, a significant part of the imperial examination system.
  • 雕舆 - Refers to ornate carriages, often associated with nobility or high status.
  • 从卫 - Refers to the entourage or guards accompanying a person of high status.
  • 甲第 - Refers to a grand mansion or residence.
  • 深邃 - Refers to a place that is deep and secluded, often used to describe a mysterious or hidden location.

Proper Nouns

  • 柳先生 - The name of the person writing the text.
  • 章子厚 - The name of the person being described in the second part of the text.
  • 河间 - The name of the place where the woman is from.
  • 京师 - The name of the capital city.
  • 省试 - The name of the examination.
  • 御街 - The name of the imperial street.
  • 雕舆 - The name of the ornate carriages.
  • 甲第 - The name of the grand mansion.
  • 深邃 - The name of the deep and secluded place.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 多造十色彩笺 - Often produced ten-colored decorative papers, referring to the production of colorful paper.
  • 涛别模新样小幅松花纸 - Xue Tao created a new style of small-sized pine flower paper, describing a specific type of paper she made.
  • 多用题诗，因寄献元公百余幅 - Used it frequently for writing poetry and sent over a hundred pieces to Yuan Zhen, indicating the purpose and quantity of the paper sent.

  • 锦江滑腻岷峨秀 - The brocade river is smooth, and Mount Min and Mount E are beautiful, a poetic description of the natural scenery.

  • 言语巧偷鹦鹉舌 - Words are skillfully stolen from the parrot's tongue, a metaphor for eloquent speech.
  • 文章分得凤凰毛 - Writing shares the phoenix's feathers, a metaphor for beautiful writing.
  • 纷纷词客皆停笔 - Many poets stop writing, indicating the high regard for Xue Tao's work.
  • 个个公侯欲梦刀 - Every nobleman desires to dream of a sword, a metaphor for aspiring to high office.
  • 别后相思隔烟水 - After separation, longing is separated by mist and water, a poetic expression of longing.
  • 富蒲花发五云高 - The rich lotus flowers bloom, reaching the height of five clouds, a poetic description of the lotus flowers.

  • 元微之赠涛诗，因寄旧诗与之云 - Yuan Wei Zhi wrote a poem to Xue Tao and sent it along with an old poem, indicating the exchange of poetry.
  • 诗篇调态人皆有，细腻风光我独知 - Everyone has poems with different styles, but I alone know the delicate beauty, a reflection on personal taste.
  • 月夜咏花怜暗淡，雨朝题柳为欹垂 - On moonlit nights, I praise flowers for their dimness; on rainy mornings, I write about willows leaning, a poetic description of nature.
  • 老大不能收拾得，与君开似教男儿 - As I grow old, I can't collect everything, but I will show it to you as if teaching a boy, a reflection on aging and sharing knowledge.

  • 薛涛好制小诗 - Literal meaning: 'Xue Tao liked to compose short poems.' Sets the context for Xue Tao's literary preferences.

  • 或以营妓无校书之号，韦南康欲奏之而罢，后遂呼之 - Ambiguous phrase likely meaning: 'Some people thought that as a courtesan, Xue Tao did not have the title of 'compiler' (校书), but Wei Nankang wanted to recommend her for the title, though it was not granted, and later people still called her by that name.' Highlights a historical misunderstanding or controversy.
  • 胡曾诗曰 - Literal meaning: 'Hu Zeng's poem says.' Indicates that the following is a poem by Hu Zeng.

  • 扫眉才子知多少，领取春风总不如 - Poetic line meaning: 'How many talented women have swept their brows (a metaphor for writing), but none can match the grace of Xue Tao in the spring breeze.' Emphasizes Xue Tao's exceptional talent.

  • 予为校书郎时，游淮泗间，张尚书宴予，酒酣，出盼盼佐欢 - Literal meaning: 'When I was a compiler (校书郎), I traveled in the Huai and Si regions, and Zhang Shangfu entertained me with a banquet. When we were drunk, he brought out Pan Pan to entertain us.' Sets the scene of Bai Juyi's encounter with Pan Pan.

  • 相思一夜知多少 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how much longing does one know in a night of thought,' expressing the depth of emotional distress.
  • 地角天涯不是長 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the ends of the earth and the far corners of the sky are not long,' implying that even the vastest distances cannot match the intensity of longing.
  • 燕子樓中思悄然 - 'In the Swallow Tower, thoughts are silent and profound,' where '燕子樓' (Swallow Tower) is a specific location associated with a story of longing and separation.
  • 自埋劍履歌塵散 - 'Since burying the sword and shoes, the songs and dust have dispersed,' a reference to the death of a lover and the fading of their memory.
  • 紅袖香銷一十年 - 'Red sleeves have faded after ten years,' a metaphor for the passage of time and the fading of beauty or memory.
  • 滿窗明月滿帘霜 - 'The full window is bathed in moonlight, and the curtain is covered in frost,' evoking a scene of solitude and coldness.
  • 被冷灯残拂卧床 - 'The quilt is cold, the lamp is dim, and the bed is brushed,' describing the physical discomfort of solitude.
  • 所以偷生尔 - 'That is why I have survived,' indicating the narrator's decision to live for the sake of her lord's reputation.
  • 黄金不惜买蛾眉，拣得如花四五枝 - 'Not sparing gold to buy beautiful women, selecting four or five like flowers,' describing the purchase of concubines.
  • 歌舞教成心力尽，一朝身去不相随 - 'Taught in dance and song until heart and strength are exhausted, yet one day the body departs without following,' expressing the fleeting nature of beauty and the inevitability of death.

  • 从死之妾 - Refers to a wife or concubine who commits suicide after the death of her husband or lord, a practice associated with the Tang Dynasty and the story of Bai Juyi and his concubine.
  • 蛾眉 - A metaphor for beautiful women, often used in classical Chinese poetry to describe concubines or mistresses.

  • 生愤懑绝倒，口不能言，颔颐而已 - 'The scholar, overwhelmed with anger and frustration, fainted; he could not speak, only nodding his head,' describing his reaction.
  • 娃前抱其颈，以绣襦拥而归于西厢 - 'The lady went forward, embraced his neck, and carried him back to the west chamber in her embroidered robe,' showing her compassion.
  • 失声长恸曰 - 'She wept aloud, saying,' expressing her deep emotion.
  • 令子一朝及此，我之罪也 - 'My dear son, how you have fallen to this state! It is my fault!' expressing her guilt.
  • 绝而复苏 - 'She fainted and then came to,' describing her emotional state.
  • 姥大骇，奔至，曰 - 'The old lady was greatly alarmed, rushed over, and said,' introducing the mother's reaction.
  • 何也 - 'What is it?' asking about the situation.
  • 某郎 - 'It is my lord,' identifying the scholar.
  • 当逐之。奈何容至此 - 'We should drive him away. How could we let him come to this?' expressing the mother's initial reaction.
  • 娃敛容却涕曰 - 'The lady composed herself, wiped away her tears, and said,' showing her composure.
  • 不然。此良家子也 - 'No. He is a gentleman from a good family,' defending the scholar.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 安邑 - Refers to Anyi, an ancient city in Shanxi Province, China.
  • 绣襦 - Refers to an embroidered robe, a symbol of wealth and status.
  • 西厢 - Refers to the west chamber, a common setting in classical Chinese literature for romantic encounters.

Proper Nouns

  • 安邑 - The name of an ancient city.
  • 绣襦 - The name of an embroidered robe.
  • 西厢 - The name of a specific room in the house.

[969]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 驅高車，持金裝 - A vivid phrase describing the act of driving a luxurious carriage and carrying valuable decorations, symbolizing wealth and status.
  • 不逾期而荡尽 - Means 'within the expected time, all is spent,' indicating rapid depletion of resources.
  • 互设诡计 - Refers to the use of various deceptive schemes.
  • 舍而逐之 - Means 'abandoning one thing to pursue another,' often with negative connotations.
  • 殆非人行 - Suggests that the actions are almost inhuman or extremely cruel.
  • 令其失志 - Means 'causing someone to lose their resolve' or 'making them lose hope.'
  • 不得齿于人伦 - Implies being excluded from the normal social order or human relations.
  • 父子之道，天性也 - Refers to the natural bond between father and son as a fundamental aspect of human nature.
  • 使其情绝 - Means 'causing the emotional bond to be severed.'
  • 杀而弃之 - Directly translates to 'killing and abandoning.'
  • 困踬若此 - Describes a state of extreme hardship or distress.
  • 欺天负人 - Means 'cheating heaven and betraying people,' implying severe moral transgression.
  • 鬼神不 - Refers to the lack of divine protection or favor.
  • 徒自遗其殃耳 - Means 'only bringing disaster upon oneself.'
  • 姥子 - Refers to a son or child of a grandmother.
  • 迨今有二十岁矣 - Means 'up until now, he has been twenty years old.'
  • 计其资，不啻值千金 - Means 'calculating his assets, they are worth no less than a thousand gold pieces.'
  • 愿计二十年衣食之用以赎身 - Means 'wishing to calculate the cost of food and clothing for twenty years to redeem himself.'
  • 当与此子别置所诣 - Means 'intending to place this child in a different location.'
  • 所诣非遥，晨昏得以温清 - Means 'the place is not far away, allowing for morning and evening care.'
  • 某愿足矣 - Means 'my wish is fulfilled.'
  • 姥度其志不可夺也 - Means 'the grandmother judged that his resolve could not be swayed.'
  • 因许之 - Means 'therefore, she agreed to it.'
  • 给姥之余，有百金 - Means 'after providing for the grandmother, there were still a hundred gold pieces left.'
  • 离北隅四五家税一隙院 - Means 'a few houses away from the northern corner, renting a small courtyard.'
  • 乃与生沐浴，易其衣服 - Means 'then he bathed him and changed his clothes.'
  • 为汤粥，通其肠 - Means 'prepared porridge to clear his intestines.'
  • 次以酥乳润其脏 - Means 'followed by butter and milk to soothe his organs.'
  • 旬余，方荐水陆之馔 - Means 'after about ten days, he was served a variety of delicacies.'
  • 头巾履袜，皆取珍异者衣之 - Means 'he was dressed in fine and rare garments.'
  • 未数月，肌肤稍腴 - Means 'within a few months, his skin became slightly plump.'
  • 卒岁，平愈如初 - Means 'by the end of the year, he had fully recovered.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高车 - Refers to a luxurious carriage, symbolizing wealth and status.
  • 金装 - Valuable decorations, often associated with opulence.
  • 人伦 - Refers to the social order and human relationships.
  • 天性 - Fundamental human nature, often used in moral and ethical contexts.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to spirits and deities, important in traditional Chinese beliefs.
  • 温清 - Morning and evening care, a term often used in filial piety contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 当昔驱高车，持金装，至某之室，不逾期而荡尽 - This sentence describes a series of actions leading to the rapid depletion of resources.
  • 且互设诡计，舍而逐之，殆非人行 - This clause describes the use of deceptive schemes and abandonment, emphasizing the inhuman nature of the actions.
  • 令其失志，不得齿于人伦 - This clause explains the consequences of the actions, leading to social exclusion.
  • 父子之道，天性也。使其情绝，杀而弃之，又困踬若此 - This sequence of clauses links the natural bond between father and son to the extreme actions taken, emphasizing the moral transgression.
  • 天下之人尽知为某也 - This clause highlights the public knowledge of the actions.
  • 生亲戚满朝，一旦当权者熟察其本末，祸将及矣 - This clause warns of the potential consequences if the actions are discovered by those in power.
  • 欺天负人，鬼神不，徒自遗其殃耳 - This clause summarizes the moral and spiritual consequences of the actions.
  • 姥度其志不可夺也，因许之 - This clause describes the grandmother's decision based on the son's resolve.
  • 给姥之余，有百金。离北隅四五家税一隙院，乃与生沐浴，易其衣服；为汤粥，通其肠；次以酥乳润其脏。旬余，方荐水陆之馔。头巾履袜，皆取珍异者衣之。未数月，肌肤稍腴；卒岁，平愈如初 - This long sequence describes the process of caring for the son and his gradual recovery.

[970]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 異時 - A time in the past, often used to introduce a narrative or description of a previous event.
  • 體已康矣，志已壯矣 - A parallel construction meaning 'the body is already healthy, the will is already strong,' emphasizing both physical and mental readiness.
  • 淵思寂慮 - Deep contemplation and silent reflection, indicating a state of intense focus.
  • 默想曩昔之藝業 - Silent recollection of past skills or knowledge, suggesting a review of previous learning.
  • 可溫習乎 - Can be reviewed or practiced again, indicating the possibility of revisiting old knowledge.
  • 十得二三耳 - Only about a third or so, indicating partial mastery of the material.
  • 命車出游 - Ordered a carriage for an outing, suggesting a leisurely or educational trip.
  • 令生拣而市之 - Ordered the student to select and purchase the books, indicating a deliberate and purposeful action.
  • 計費百金 - Costing a hundred gold pieces, indicating a significant financial investment.
  • 盡載以歸 - Loaded everything and returned home, suggesting the completion of the task.
  • 斥棄百慮以志學 - Discarded all other concerns and focused on learning, indicating a dedication to study.
  • 俾夜作昼 - Worked through the night as if it were day, indicating intense and continuous effort.
  • 孜孜 - Diligently, indicating persistent and hard work.
  • 偶坐 - Sat together, indicating a shared or companionable activity.
  • 宵分乃寐 - Slept only after midnight, indicating late-night activities.
  • 伺其疲倦 - Waited for him to become tired, indicating attentive care.
  • 谕之缀诗赋 - Advised him to compose poetry and prose, indicating guidance in creative writing.
  • 策名試藝 - Registered for the imperial examination, indicating a formal step in the educational process.
  • 未也，且令精熟，以俟百戰 - Not yet, but let him become more proficient to prepare for many battles, indicating a need for further preparation.
  • 一上登甲科 - Passed the highest level of the imperial examination on his first attempt, indicating exceptional achievement.
  • 聲振禮闌 - His reputation spread widely, indicating widespread recognition.
  • 斂衽喜躍 - Bowing and jumping for joy, indicating great admiration and excitement.
  • 願友之而不得 - Wanted to be his friend but could not, indicating high regard and respect.
  • 今秀士苟得一科招一第 - Nowadays, talented scholars who pass one examination and get one degree, indicating a common but limited achievement.
  • 連衡多士，爭霸群英 - Compete with many scholars and vie for supremacy among the elite, indicating a competitive and ambitious goal.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 甲科 - The highest level of the imperial examination, indicating the pinnacle of academic achievement.
  • 禮闌 - The end of the imperial examination period, indicating the conclusion of the examination season.
  • 秀士 - Talented scholars, indicating individuals with high academic and moral standards.
  • 一科招一第 - Passing one examination and getting one degree, indicating a common but limited achievement in the imperial examination system.

Proper Nouns

  • 甲科 - The highest level of the imperial examination.
  • 禮闌 - The end of the imperial examination period.
  • 秀士 - Talented scholars.

[971]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 将之官 - About to take office or going to assume an official position.
  • 子若弃我，当自刭以就死 - If you abandon me, I will kill myself to die. The phrase '自刭' (zijing) specifically means 'to commit suicide by cutting one's throat.'
  • 固辞不从 - Firmly refused to comply or steadfastly declined.
  • 勤请弥恳 - Persistently and earnestly requested or kept on pleading with great earnestness.
  • 当令我回 - I will make you return or I will have you come back.
  • 许诺 - Agreed or promised.
  • 除书至 - An imperial edict arrived or a decree was issued.
  • 拜成都尹，兼剑甫采访使 - Was appointed as the Governor of Chengdu and concurrently as the Inspector of Jianfu or was promoted to the position of Governor of Chengdu and Inspector of Jianfu.
  • 见其祖父官讳 - Saw the names of his ancestors and their official titles or recognized the names of his ancestors and their titles.
  • 抚背恸哭移时 - Embraced his back and wept bitterly for a long time or hugged him and cried for a long while.
  • 中外婚媾 - Marriages within and outside the family or both inner and outer marriages.
  • 自黩 - To defile oneself or to disgrace oneself.
  • 命媒氏通二姓之好 - Ordered the matchmaker to arrange the marriage between the two families or instructed the matchmaker to facilitate the union of the two families.
  • 备六札以迎之 - Prepared six sets of documents to welcome her or arranged for six formal documents to greet her.
  • 遂如秦晋之偶 - Thus they became a couple like Qin and Jin or therefore they were united as a couple, like Qin and Jin.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成都尹 - The title of the governor of Chengdu, a major city in Sichuan Province.
  • 剑甫采访使 - The title of the inspector of Jianfu, a region in Sichuan.
  • 常州 - A city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 秦晋之偶 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'a couple like Qin and Jin,' referring to the historical alliance between the states of Qin and Jin during the Spring and Autumn period.

Proper Nouns

  • 娃 - A term of endearment for the female character.
  • 生 - A respectful form of address for the male character.
  • 成都 - The city of Chengdu in Sichuan Province.
  • 剑甫 - The region of Jianfu in Sichuan.
  • 常州 - The city of Changzhou in Jiangsu Province.
  • 秦 - The state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 晋 - The state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period.

[972]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 岁时伏腊 - Traditional Chinese festivals of Vassa (Buddhist observance) and Sacrifice (ancestor worship), indicating a period of observance and reverence.
  • 妇道甚修 - Means 'the woman's conduct was very refined,' emphasizing her adherence to proper feminine virtues.
  • 治家严整 - Means 'she managed the household with strict order,' highlighting her organizational skills.
  • 极为亲所眷尚 - Means 'she was greatly favored by her relatives,' indicating her high regard within the family.
  • 持孝甚至 - Means 'she practiced filial piety to an extreme degree,' emphasizing her dedication to mourning her parents.
  • 灵芝产于倚庐 - Refers to the miraculous growth of lingzhi mushrooms in the mourning hut, symbolizing divine approval or good fortune.
  • 一穗三秀 - Means 'one stalk with three heads,' a rare and auspicious phenomenon.
  • 白燕数十，巢其屋薨 - Describes the appearance of dozens of white swans nesting in the house, symbolizing purity and nobility.
  • 宠锡加等 - Means 'the imperial favor and gifts were increased,' indicating special recognition from the emperor.
  • 累迁清显之任 - Means 'she was repeatedly promoted to high-ranking positions,' highlighting her career advancement.
  • 封沂国夫人 - Means 'she was enfeoffed as the Lady of Yiyu,' indicating her noble title.
  • 四子，皆为大官 - Means 'she had four sons, all of whom became high-ranking officials,' emphasizing her family's success.
  • 内外隆盛，莫之与京 - Means 'both inside and outside the family, prosperity was unmatched,' emphasizing the family's wealth and status.
  • 娼荡之姬，节行如是 - Means 'a courtesan who behaved with such virtue,' highlighting the contrast between her background and her conduct.
  • 虽古先烈女，不能逾也 - Means 'even ancient virtuous women could not surpass her,' emphasizing her exceptional virtue.
  • 焉得不为之叹息哉 - Means 'how can one not sigh at this,' expressing admiration and respect.
  • 叛臣辱妇，每出于名门世族 - Means 'traitors and disgraced wives often come from noble families,' contrasting with the courtesan's virtue.
  • 伶工贱女，乃有洁白坚贞之行 - Means 'even lowly performers and courtesans can exhibit pure and steadfast conduct,' emphasizing the unexpected virtue of those from humble backgrounds.
  • 岂非秉彝之良，有不问耶 - Means 'is this not due to the inherent goodness of humanity, regardless of social status?' expressing a philosophical reflection.
  • 诚出天性之所安，固非激以干名也 - Means 'this is truly a matter of innate nature, not something done to gain fame,' emphasizing the authenticity of the actions.
  • 娃之守志不乱，卒相其夫，以底于荣美 - Means 'the courtesan remained steadfast in her resolve, ultimately supporting her husband to achieve honor and beauty,' highlighting her unwavering commitment.
  • 则尤人所难 - Means 'which is particularly difficult for others,' emphasizing the exceptional nature of her conduct.
  • 娼也犹然，士乎可以知所勉矣 - Means 'even a courtesan can do this, so how much more should scholars strive to do so,' concluding with a call to action for scholars.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岁时伏腊 - Traditional Chinese festivals.
  • 妇道 - Feminine virtues and conduct.
  • 治家严整 - Household management.
  • 极为亲所眷尚 - Family favor.
  • 持孝甚至 - Filial piety.
  • 灵芝 - Symbolic of good fortune and divine approval.
  • 白燕 - Symbolic of purity and nobility.
  • 宠锡加等 - Imperial favor and gifts.
  • 累迁清显之任 - Career advancement.
  • 封沂国夫人 - Noble title.
  • 四子，皆为大官 - Family success.
  • 内外隆盛，莫之与京 - Prosperity and status.
  • 娼荡之姬，节行如是 - Virtue of a courtesan.
  • 虽古先烈女，不能逾也 - Comparison to ancient virtuous women.
  • 焉得不为之叹息哉 - Expression of admiration.
  • 叛臣辱妇，每出于名门世族 - Contrast with traitors and disgraced wives.
  • 伶工贱女，乃有洁白坚贞之行 - Virtue of lowly performers and courtesans.
  • 岂非秉彝之良，有不问耶 - Philosophical reflection on human nature.
  • 诚出天性之所安，固非激以干名也 - Authenticity of actions.
  • 娃之守志不乱，卒相其夫，以底于荣美 - Unwavering commitment.
  • 则尤人所难 - Exceptional nature of her conduct.
  • 娼也犹然，士乎可以知所勉矣 - Call to action for scholars.

[973]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 殊色 - A term used to describe exceptional beauty or appearance.
  • 冶容自喜 - Means to take pride in one's own beauty or appearance.
  • 引满尽欢 - Means to drink to one's heart's content and enjoy oneself fully.
  • 亡生破产 - Means to lose one's life or go bankrupt, indicating extreme devotion or obsession.
  • 冠诸籍中 - Means to be listed or ranked among the best in a register or directory.
  • 大售于时 - Means to be highly popular or successful in one's time.
  • 贵游子 - Refers to a wealthy and influential person, often a noble or high-ranking official.
  • 戚里女 - Refers to a woman from the imperial clan or a noble family.
  • 遇帅甚悍 - Means to treat the general very harshly or cruelly.
  • 取死白刃下 - Means to agree to die under the blade of a sword, indicating a death threat.
  • 幼贵 - Means to be young and wealthy or privileged.
  • 喜淫 - Means to have a fondness for or inclination towards sexual pleasure.
  • 内苦其妻 - Means to be troubled or oppressed by one's wife internally.
  • 阴出重赂 - Means to secretly offer a large bribe.
  • 削去娼之籍 - Means to remove the prostitute's name from the official register.
  • 挈之南海 - Means to take her to the South Sea (Guangdong region).
  • 馆之他舍 - Means to house her in another place or residence.
  • 公余而同 - Means to meet or be together during leisure time.
  • 夕隐而归 - Means to hide or return home in the evening.
  • 雅有慧性 - Means to have a naturally intelligent or perceptive disposition.
  • 事帅尤谨 - Means to serve the general with particular diligence or care.
  • 平居以女职自守 - Means to conduct oneself according to the duties of a woman, maintaining propriety.
  • 非其理不妄发 - Means to refrain from speaking or acting without reason or justification.
  • 厚帅之左右 - Means to treat the general's subordinates or attendants well.
  • 咸能得其欢心 - Means to all be able to win their favor or approval.
  • 益嬖之 - Means to become increasingly favored or cherished.
  • 间岁 - Means after a year or so.
  • 思一见娼 - Means to want to see the prostitute once more.
  • 惮其妻 - Means to fear or be hesitant due to his wife.
  • 素与监军使厚 - Means to have a close relationship with the military commander.
  • 密遣道意 - Means to secretly send a message or express an idea.
  • 为方略 - Means to devise a plan or strategy.
  • 给其妻 - Means to tell or inform his wife.
  • 瘳当速矣 - Means that recovery will be quick.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长安里中 - Refers to the streets or neighborhoods of Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), the capital city during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 娼 - Refers to a prostitute, a common figure in ancient Chinese literature and society.
  • 王公矩人豪客 - Refers to high-ranking officials, wealthy merchants, and influential guests who frequented such gatherings.
  • 岭南帅甲 - Refers to a powerful general stationed in the southern region (Lingnan, modern-day Guangdong and Guangxi).
  • 监军使 - Refers to a military commander who oversees and supervises military operations.
  • 瘳 - Refers to recovery from illness.
  • 白刃 - Refers to a sword or blade, symbolizing death or danger.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨娼 - The name of the prostitute.
  • 长安 - The capital city during the Tang Dynasty, modern-day Xi'an.
  • 岭南 - The southern region, modern-day Guangdong and Guangxi.
  • 帅甲 - The name of the powerful general.
  • 监军使 - The title of the military commander.

[974]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 某有善婢 - I have a good maid; '某' is a humble first-person pronoun.
  • 久给事贵室 - Has long served in a noble household; '给事' means 'to serve' and '贵室' refers to a high-status household.
  • 动得人意 - Always pleases the master; '动' means 'always' and '得人意' means 'pleases the master.'
  • 请夫人听以婢安将军四体，如何？” - Please let the lady hear that the maid can ease the general's body, what do you think?; '安将军四体' means 'ease the general's body.'
  • 中贵人言仁也 - The eunuch speaks of benevolence; '中贵人' refers to a high-ranking eunuch and '言仁' means 'speaks of benevolence.'
  • 果然，于吾无苦耳，可促召婢来 - If it is true, it will cause no trouble for me, so please quickly summon the maid; '果然' means 'if it is true' and '于吾无苦耳' means 'it will cause no trouble for me.'
  • 计未行而事泄 - The plan had not yet been carried out when it was revealed; '计未行' means 'the plan had not yet been carried out' and '事泄' means 'it was revealed.'
  • 帅之妻乃拥健婢数十，列白挺，炽膏镬于庭而伺之矣 - The general's wife then gathered dozens of strong maids, lined up with white spears, and heated oil in a cauldron in the courtyard, waiting for her; '拥健婢数十' means 'gathered dozens of strong maids,' '列白挺' means 'lined up with white spears,' '炽膏镬于庭' means 'heated oil in a cauldron in the courtyard,' and '伺之矣' means 'waiting for her.'
  • 须其至，当投之沸鬲 - When she arrives, she will be thrown into the boiling cauldron; '须其至' means 'when she arrives' and '投之沸鬲' means 'thrown into the boiling cauldron.'
  • 帅闻而大恐，促命止之 - The general heard and was greatly frightened, and quickly ordered to stop it; '闻而大恐' means 'heard and was greatly frightened' and '促命止之' means 'quickly ordered to stop it.'
  • 此自我意，几累于渠 - This was my own idea, almost implicating her; '自我意' means 'my own idea' and '几累于渠' means 'almost implicating her.'
  • 今幸吾之未死也，必使脱其虎喙 - Now that I am still alive, I will definitely make her escape from the tiger's mouth; '今幸吾之未死也' means 'Now that I am still alive' and '必使脱其虎喙' means 'I will definitely make her escape from the tiger's mouth.'
  • 不然，且无及矣 - Otherwise, it will be too late; '不然' means 'otherwise' and '且无及矣' means 'it will be too late.'
  • 乃大遗其奇宝，命家童榜轻舫，卫娼北归 - He then gave her a large amount of rare treasures, ordered his servants to paddle a light boat, and escorted the prostitute northward; '大遗其奇宝' means 'gave her a large amount of rare treasures,' '命家童榜轻舫' means 'ordered his servants to paddle a light boat,' and '卫娼北归' means 'escorted the prostitute northward.'
  • 自是帅之愤益振，不逾旬而物故 - From then on, the general's anger grew stronger, and he died within a few days; '自是帅之愤益振' means 'From then on, the general's anger grew stronger' and '不逾旬而物故' means 'he died within a few days.'
  • 而娼之行适及洪矣 - And the prostitute's journey just reached Hong; '适及洪矣' means 'just reached Hong.'
  • 闻至，娼乃尽返帅之赂，设位而哭曰 - When she heard, the prostitute returned all the general's gifts, set up a place, and wept, saying; '闻至' means 'When she heard,' '尽返帅之赂' means 'returned all the general's gifts,' and '设位而哭曰' means 'set up a place and wept, saying.'
  • 将军由妾而卒，将军且死，妾安用生为？ - The general died because of me, the general is about to die, why should I live?; '由妾而卒' means 'died because of me' and '妾安用生为' means 'why should I live?'
  • 妾岂孤将军者耶 - Am I the one who abandoned the general?; '岂孤将军者耶' means 'Am I the one who abandoned the general?'
  • 即撤奠而死之 - Immediately she removed the offerings and died; '撤奠' means 'removed the offerings' and '死之' means 'died.'
  • 夫娼，以色事人者也 - Prostitutes are those who serve people with their beauty; '以色事人者也' means 'serve people with their beauty.'
  • 非其利则不合矣 - If it is not in their interest, they will not agree; '非其利则不合矣' means 'If it is not in their interest, they will not agree.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 某有善婢，久给事贵室，动得人意 - This clause introduces the maid and her qualities, with '动得人意' modifying '善婢.'
  • 请夫人听以婢安将军四体，如何？” - The sentence is elliptical, with '以婢安将军四体' being the object of '听.'
  • 计未行而事泄 - The sentence is compact, with '计未行' and '事泄' forming a concessive relationship.
  • 帅之妻乃拥健婢数十，列白挺，炽膏镬于庭而伺之矣 - The sentence is complex, with '拥健婢数十' and '列白挺' forming a parallel structure, followed by '炽膏镬于庭而伺之矣.'
  • 须其至，当投之沸鬲 - The sentence is conditional, with '须其至' introducing the condition and '当投之沸鬲' the consequence.
  • 帅闻而大恐，促命止之 - The sentence is elliptical, with '促命止之' being the object of '大恐.'
  • 此自我意，几累于渠 - The sentence is concessive, with '此自我意' introducing the concession and '几累于渠' the consequence.
  • 今幸吾之未死也，必使脱其虎喙 - The sentence is conditional, with '今幸吾之未死也' introducing the condition and '必使脱其虎喙' the consequence.
  • 乃大遗其奇宝，命家童榜轻舫 - The sentence is incomplete, but it suggests that he gave her a large amount of rare treasures and ordered his servants to paddle a light boat.

  • 洛阳无限青楼女，袖掩红牙金凤缕 - Description of the many courtesans in Luoyang, with their sleeves hiding their instruments (红牙金凤缕, a type of musical instrument); can be translated as 'In Luoyang, there are countless courtesans, their sleeves concealing their red ivory and golden phoenix instruments.'
  • 春衫粉面谁家郎、只把黄金买歌舞 - Description of a young man (谁家郎) who buys entertainment (歌舞) with gold, suggesting a life of luxury and indulgence; can be rendered as 'The young man in his spring robe and powdered face, spends gold only to buy songs and dances.'
  • 就中薄幸五陵儿，一日怜新弃如土 - Reference to the '五陵儿' (young men from the Five Lings), who are fickle and discard their lovers quickly; can be translated as 'Among them, the fickle young men of the Five Lings, who in one day cherish and then cast aside like dirt.'

  • 浣花溪上海棠湾，薛涛朱户皆金环 - Description of the picturesque 浣花溪 (Huanhua Creek) and 海棠湾 (Haitang Bay), with the homes of 薛涛 (a famous poetess) adorned with golden rings; can be translated as 'By the Huanhua Creek, in the Haitang Bay, the homes of Xue Tao are adorned with golden rings.'
  • 韦皋笔逸玳瑁落，张祜盏滑琉璃干 - Reference to 韦皋 (a famous calligrapher) and 张祜 (a poet), suggesting their artistic brilliance; can be rendered as 'Wei Gao's brushwork is effortless, leaving behind the elegance of tortoiseshell, while Zhang Hu's wine cup is smooth as glass.'

  • 疏影横斜安在哉？ - Poetic question asking where the sparse, slanting shadows of the plum blossoms have gone.

  • 谢希孟者，陆象山门人也 - This phrase means 'Xie Ximeng was a disciple of Lu Xiangshan.'
  • 少豪俊 - This phrase means 'he was young and heroic.'

  • 他日复为妓造鸳鸯楼，象山又以为言 - This phrase means 'later, he built a pavilion called the Swallow Tower for the courtesan, and Lu Xiangshan criticized him again.'
  • 希孟谢曰：“非特建楼，且为作记。” - This phrase means 'Xie Ximeng replied, 'Not only did I build the pavilion, but I also wrote an inscription for it.''

  • 自逊、抗、机、云之死，而天地英灵之气，不钟于男子而钟于妇人 - This phrase means 'Since the deaths of Xun, Kang, Ji, and Yun, the divine and talented energy of heaven and earth has not been concentrated in men but in women.'
  • 象山默然，知其侮也 - This phrase means 'Lu Xiangshan remained silent, knowing that Xie Ximeng was mocking him.'
  • 双桨浪花平，夹岸青山锁 - This phrase describes a scene of a boat with two oars, the waves are calm, and the green mountains on both sides are locked together.
  • 你自归家我自归，说着何如过 - This phrase means 'You go home, and I go home; what should we say to pass the time?'
  • 我断不思量，你莫思量我 - This phrase means 'I will not think of you, and you should not think of me.'
  • 将你从前与我心，再傍他人可 - This phrase means 'If you once had a heart for me, can you now turn to another person?'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '遂靈公嫡母襄夫人宣美' is unclear due to the character ',' which may be a typo or corruption. The intended meaning might be that he 'proclaimed the beauty of Duke Ling's principal wife, Lady Xiang.'
  • The phrase '柔公之夫人南子' is ambiguous. '柔' could mean 'flattered' or 'caressed,' but the context suggests 'flattered.'
  • The phrase '既自晋归宋' is elliptical. It means 'after returning from Jin to Song,' but the sentence structure is compact.
  • The phrase '太子蒯献孟于齐' is elliptical. It means 'Prince Kuai sent Meng to Qi,' but the sentence structure is compact.
  • The phrase '过来野，野人歌之曰' is elliptical. It means 'came to the countryside, where the villagers sang, saying,' but the sentence structure is compact.
  • The proverbial saying '既定尔娄猪，盍归我艾' is ambiguous. '娄猪' likely refers to a pig, and '艾' could mean 'cabbage' or 'lettuce.' The intended meaning might be 'Having settled your pig, why not return my cabbage?'
  • The phrase '惧欲走，公闭门而位之，目尽肿' is ambiguous. '位之' might mean 'placed him in a position' or 'left him standing,' and '目尽肿' means 'his eyes were swollen from crying or anger.'
  • The phrase '窃驾君车，罪刚' is unclear. '罪刚' might mean 'the crime was severe' or 'the punishment was harsh.'

  • 遂冊免明 - Means 'thus, the emperor issued a decree to dismiss Ming'; indicates the formal removal of Ming from his position.

  • 逆禍迫切 - Means 'the disaster was imminent'; indicates the urgency of the situation.
  • 賴宗廟神靈，董賢等以聞 - Means 'thanks to the divine protection of the ancestors, Dong Xian and others reported it'; indicates the role of divine intervention and the actions of Dong Xian.
  • 咸伏其辜 - Means 'all were punished for their crimes'; indicates the punishment of those involved.

  • 因醫技進，幾危社稷 - Means 'through his medical skills, he nearly endangered the state'; indicates the potential threat posed by Hong.
  • 朕以恭皇后故不忍有雲 - Means 'I, due to the respect for Empress Gong, cannot speak of this'; indicates the emperor's reluctance to criticize Hong due to family ties.

  • 上骠骑将军印绶，罢归就第 - Means 'to remove the rank of Grand Marshal of Cavalry, and to retire to one's home.'

[1017]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雖為三公，常給事中領尚書 - Although holding the position of Grand Councillor, he often served as an attendant in the palace and oversaw the Shangshu (Chancellery).
  • 百官因賢奏事 - All officials submitted their reports through him.
  • 徙為光禄大夫，秩中二千石 - He was transferred to the position of Grand Officer of the Palace, with a rank equivalent to 2,000 measures of grain.
  • 弟宽信代賢為驸馬都尉 - His younger brother Kuanxin succeeded him as the Imperial Son-in-Law.
  • 董氏親屬皆侍中諸曹奉朝請，寵在丁、傅之右矣 - Members of the Dong family were all appointed as Attendants and Ministers, receiving regular invitations to court, and their favor surpassed that of the Ding and Fu families.
  • 宴見，群臣在前，單于怪賢年少 - During a banquet, with all officials present, the Chanyu was surprised by the young age of Dong.
  • 以問譯 - He asked the interpreter.
  • 上令譯報曰 - The emperor instructed the interpreter to reply.
  • 大司馬年少，以大賢居位 - The Grand Marshal is young, but he holds a position due to his great virtue.
  • 乃起，拜賀漢朝得賢臣 - He then stood up and bowed, congratulating the Han court on having a virtuous minister.
  • 初，丞相孔光為御史大夫，時賢父恭為御史，事光 - Initially, Chancellor Kong Guang was the Grand Censor, and at that time, Dong's father Gong was also a Censor, serving under Kong Guang.
  • 及賢為大司馬，與光並為三公 - When Dong became the Grand Marshal, he and Kong Guang were both among the Three Excellencies.
  • 上故令賢私過光 - The emperor intentionally had Dong visit Kong Guang privately.
  • 光雅恭謹，知上欲尊寵賢 - Kong Guang, being naturally respectful and cautious, understood that the emperor intended to honor Dong.
  • 及聞賢當來也，光警戒衣冠，出門待望 - When he heard that Dong was coming, Kong Guang dressed formally and waited outside.
  • 見賢車，乃卻入 - Seeing Dong's carriage, he retreated inside.
  • 賢至中門，光入閣 - When Dong reached the middle gate, Kong Guang entered the hall.
  • 既下車，乃出拜謁 - After Dong had alighted from his carriage, he stepped out to pay respects.
  • 送迎甚謹，不敢以賓客均敵之禮 - They received and saw him off with great respect,不敢 using the same courteous treatment as for equals.
  • 賢歸，上聞之喜，立拜光兩兄子為大夫、常侍 - When Dong returned, the emperor, upon hearing about it, was pleased and immediately appointed Kong Guang's two nephews as Attendants and Regular Attendants.
  • 賢由是權與人主侔矣 - From then on, Dong's power was equal to that of the emperor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三公 - Refers to the highest-ranking officials in the Han government, typically including the Chancellor, the Grand Marshal, and the Grand Commandant.
  • 給事中 - A position in the imperial court that involved advising the emperor and overseeing various administrative tasks.
  • 尚書 - The Shangshu (Chancellery) was a central government institution responsible for drafting and issuing imperial edicts.
  • 光祿大夫 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, often involved in ceremonial duties.
  • 駙馬都尉 - A position held by the emperor's sons-in-law, often involving military and administrative responsibilities.
  • 侍中 - A position in the imperial court that involved serving as an attendant to the emperor and participating in important state affairs.
  • 諸曹 - Refers to various departments or offices within the imperial administration.
  • 奉朝請 - A regular invitation to attend court, often a sign of favor and importance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 莽使谒者以太后诏，即阙下册贤曰 - Wang Mang sent a court official to deliver the Empress Dowager's edict, and at the palace gate, he formally demoted the Virtuous One, saying
  • 间者以来，阴阳不调，灾害并臻，元元蒙辜 - Since then, the yin and yang have been out of balance, disasters have occurred simultaneously, and the common people have suffered
  • 高安侯贤未更事理，为大司马，不合众心，非所以折冲绥远也 - The Marquis of Gao'an, the Virtuous One, has not experienced state affairs, serving as Grand Marshal, and is not in harmony with the people's hearts; this is not how to repel the enemy and pacify the distant
  • 其收司马印绶，罢归第 - Seize the seal and sash of the Grand Marshal, and dismiss him from his post, returning him to his residence

  • 莽疑其诈，有司奏请发贤棺至狱诊视 - Wang Mang suspected it was a fraud, and the officials proposed to open the Virtuous One's coffin at the prison for examination
  • 莽复讽大司徒光奏 - Wang Mang then indirectly instructed the Grand Tutor Guang to submit a report
  • 贤质性巧佞，翼好以获封侯 - The Virtuous One's nature is cunning and sycophantic, and he gained his title as Marquis through flattery
  • 父子专朝，兄弟并宠 - Father and son monopolized the court, and brothers shared favor
  • 多受赐，治第宅，造家扩，放效无极，不异玉制，费以万万计，国家为空虚 - He received many gifts, built mansions, constructed family estates, imitated extravagance without limit, differing from the imperial standards, spending millions of units, leaving the state impoverished
  • 父子骄麦，至不为使者礼，受赐不拜，罪恶暴著 - Father and son were arrogant, to the point of not showing respect to envoys, not bowing when receiving gifts, with their crimes and vices openly evident

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 禁止賢不得入宫殿司马中 - This phrase is elliptical, implying that Wang Xun was prevented from entering the palace in his role as Grand Marshal.
  • 賢不知所为，诣闭免冠徒跣谢 - This sequence describes Wang Xun's actions in response to the prohibition, but the exact order of events is not clear.
  • 莽使谒者以太后诏，即阙下册贤曰 - This clause is elliptical, omitting the specific content of the edict.
  • 间者以来，阴阳不调，灾害并臻，元元蒙辜 - This sequence lists a series of calamities, but the exact relationship between them is not specified.
  • 高安侯贤未更事理，为大司马，不合众心，非所以折冲绥远也 - This clause is a formal critique of Wang Xun's suitability for the position of Grand Marshal.
  • 其收司马印绶，罢归第 - This clause is elliptical, omitting the specific details of the demotion.
  • 即日，贤与妻皆自杀，家惶恐夜葬 - This sequence describes the immediate aftermath of the demotion, but the exact timing and circumstances are not clear.
  • 莽疑其诈，有司奏请发贤棺至狱诊视 - This clause is elliptical, omitting the specific reasons for the examination.
  • 莽复讽大司徒光奏 - This clause is elliptical, omitting the specific content of the report.
  • 贤质性巧佞，翼好以获封侯 - This clause is a formal critique of Wang Xun's character and rise to power.
  • 父子专朝，兄弟并宠 - This clause is a formal critique of the Wang family's monopolization of power.
  • 多受赐，治第宅，造家扩，放效无极，不异玉制，费以万万计，国家为空虚 - This clause is a formal critique of the Wang family's extravagance and its impact on the state.
  • 父子骄麦，至不为使者礼，受赐不拜，罪恶暴著 - This clause is a formal critique of the Wang family's arrogance and disrespect.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 竟以妆饰妖丽，巧言取容 - Ultimately using elaborate and alluring adornments, and clever words to win favor, describing how these officials gained the emperor's trust.
  • 董贤以雾绡革衣，飘若蝉翼 - Fine gauze and leather garments, light as a cicada's wing, highlighting Dong Xian's elegance and grace.
  • 帝入燕息之房，命筵卿易轻衣小袖，不用奢带修裙，故使宛转便易也 - The emperor's command to Dong Xian to change into lighter clothing and shorter sleeves, emphasizing the emperor's affection and the intimate nature of their relationship.
  • 宫人皆效其断袖 - All the palace women imitated his practice of cutting off his sleeve, referring to the famous 'Dong Xian's Cut Sleeve' story, where the emperor cut off his sleeve to avoid waking Dong Xian.

  • 愈益爱怜子 - Describes deepening affection, meaning 'He loved and pitied Zigao even more.'
  • 肤理色泽，柔靡都曼 - Describes physical beauty, meaning 'His skin, texture, and color were soft, delicate, and beautiful.'
  • 猿臂善骑射，上下若风 - Describes agility and skill, meaning 'He had long arms like a monkey, excelled at riding and archery, and moved swiftly like the wind.'
  • 性恭谨，恒执佩身刀及待酒炙 - Describes behavior and duties, meaning 'He was naturally respectful and cautious, always holding his dagger and waiting to serve wine and roast meat.'
  • 性急，有所恚，目若虎，焰焰欲咬人 - Describes intense anger, meaning 'He was quick-tempered and when angry, his eyes looked like a tiger's, burning with the desire to bite.'
  • 见子高则立解 - Describes the calming effect of Zigao's presence, meaning 'Seeing Zigao, he immediately calmed down.'
  • 子高亦曲意附会得其欢 - Describes efforts to gain approval, meaning 'Zigao also went out of his way to please him and gained his favor.'
  • 尝为诗赠之曰 - Introduces a poem, meaning 'He once wrote a poem to give him, saying.'
  • 昔闻周小史，今歌明下童 - Refers to historical and contemporary figures, meaning 'In the past, I heard of the Zhou historian; now I sing of the bright young boy.'
  • 玉廛手不别，羊车市若空 - Describes a lack of distinction or value, meaning 'The jade market is indistinguishable, the sheep cart market is empty.'
  • 谁愁两雄并，金貂应让侬 - A statement of confidence and rivalry, meaning 'Who would be concerned if two heroes were together? The golden fox should yield to me.'
  • 人言吾有帝王相，审尔，当册汝为后，但恐同姓致嫌耳 - A conditional statement with a hint of concern, meaning 'People say I have the appearance of a king or emperor. If that is true, I will make you my queen, but I fear that being of the same surname might cause jealousy.'
  • 古有女主，当亦有男后 - A historical reference to gender roles, meaning 'In ancient times there were female rulers, so there should also be male consorts.'
  • 明公果垂异恩，奴亦何辞作吴孟子耶 - A humble and respectful response, meaning 'If you truly bestow this special favor, I will not refuse to be your Fuzi (a term of respect).'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 願得炎州無霜色，出入歡袖百千年 - Poetic expression wishing for a place to be free of frost and for joy to last for centuries. '出入歡袖' is metaphorical, suggesting joy should be present both inside and outside, or in all aspects of life.
  • 事漸泄，所不知者司空而已 - Statement indicating the matter was gradually revealed, and only the official (司空) was unaware. '所不知者' is a nominalized clause meaning 'the one who does not know.'
  • 會王僧辯有母喪，未及為禮娶 - Statement about Wang Sengbian having a mother's death and not yet having time to perform the proper mourning rites before marriage. '未及為禮' means 'not yet had time to perform the proper rites.'
  • 忿恨以语僧辩，用他事停司空女婚 - Statement about Zihao expressing anger to Wang Sengbian and using another matter to stop the marriage of the official's daughter. '用他事' means 'using another matter.'
  • 司空怒，且谓僧辩之见图也 - Statement about the official being angry and believing that Wang Sengbian was plotting against him. '见图' means 'to plot against.'
  • 遂发兵袭僧辩并其子，缢杀之 - Statement about the official sending troops to attack Wang Sengbian and his son, killing them by strangulation. '缢杀' means 'killed by strangulation.'
  • 率子高实为军锋焉 - Statement about Zihao leading the troops as the vanguard. '军锋' means 'vanguard of the army.'
  • 自是子高引避不敢人 - Statement about Zihao then avoiding and not daring to approach. '引避' means 'to avoid.'
  • 知之，仍领子高之镇 - Statement about someone knowing about it and still leading Zihao to the town. '领子高之镇' means 'leading Zihao to the town.'
  • 女以念极，结气死 - Statement about the official's daughter dying from excessive grief. '结气死' means 'died from grief.'
  • 司空为武帝崩，后从犹子入嗣大统 - Statement about the official becoming the successor to the throne after the death of Emperor Wu, following his nephew. '嗣大统' means 'successor to the throne.'
  • 子高为右卫将军散骑常侍，积功封文招县子 - Statement about Zihao becoming a Right Guard General and a Scattered Rider Attendant, accumulating merit and being granted the title of Marquis of Wenzhao. '积功封' means 'accumulating merit and being granted a title.'
  • 废帝时，坐诬谋反诛 - Statement about Zihao being executed during the reign of the deposed emperor for falsely plotting a rebellion. '坐诬谋反' means 'being executed for falsely plotting a rebellion.'
  • 人以为隐报焉 - Statement about people believing it was a hidden retribution. '隐报' means 'hidden retribution.'
  • 王韶字德茂，少美丽，善姿首 - Statement about Wang Shao, whose name is Dezhao, and who was handsome and well-formed. '善姿首' means 'well-formed appearance.'
  • 初袭父封都乡侯，为太子舍人，累迁郢州刺史 - Statement about Wang Shao initially inheriting his father's title of Duke of Duxiang, serving as a Gentleman of the Crown Prince's Household, and later being promoted to Governor of Yingshou. '袭父封' means 'inheriting his father's title.'
  • 韶其为幼童，庾开府信爱之，有断袖之欢，衣食所资，皆信所给 - Statement about Wang Shao, when he was a young child, being loved by Yu Kai-fu and having a close relationship, with Yu providing for his clothing and food. '断袖之欢' means 'a close relationship.'
  • 遇客，韶亦为信侍酒 - Statement about when guests were present, Wang Shao also served as a companion to Yu. '侍酒' means 'to serve as a companion.'
  • 后为郢州 - Statement about Wang Shao later becoming the Governor of Yingshou.
  • 信西上江陵，途经江夏，韶接信甚薄，坐青油幕下，引信入宴，坐信别榻，有自矜色 - Statement about Yu traveling west to Jiangling, passing through Jiangxia, where Wang Shao received him poorly, seating him separately under a blue oil canopy, and showing a self-important demeanor. '青油幕' means 'blue oil canopy.'
  • 宾客满座，韶甚惭耻 - Statement about guests filling the room, and Wang Shao feeling very ashamed. '宾客满座' means 'guests filling the room.'

  • 守者怠而假寐 - The guards grew lazy and fell into a light sleep.
  • 忽若有物惊悟者，即已失妻矣 - Suddenly, something startled them awake, and his wife was already gone.
  • 关扃如故，莫知所出 - The doors and locks were as they were, and no one knew where she had gone.
  • 出门山脸，咫尺迷闷，不可寻逐 - Outside the door, the mountain face was nearby, but it was confusing and impossible to search.
  • 迨明，纥无其迹 - By dawn, there was no trace of her.
  • 绝大愤痛，誓不徒还 - He was extremely angry and sad, vowing not to return without his wife.
  • 因辞疾，驻其军 - He feigned illness and stayed with his troops.
  • 日往四通，即深凌险以索之 - Every day, he went in all directions, delving deep into the dangerous terrain to search for her.
  • 既逾月，忽于百里之外丛问，得其妻绣履一只 - After more than a month, he suddenly found one of his wife's embroidered shoes in a dense forest a hundred miles away.
  • 虽浸雨濡，犹可辨识 - Although it was soaked with rain, it could still be recognized.
  • 纥尤凄悼，求之益坚 - Ouyang He was especially sad and determined to find her even more.
  • 选壮士三十人，持兵负粮，岩栖野食 - He selected thirty strong men, armed with weapons and carrying food, to live in caves and eat in the wild.
  • 又旬余，远所舍约二百里，南望一山，葱秀迥出 - After another ten days, they were about two hundred miles away from their camp. Looking south, they saw a mountain standing out prominently.
  • 至其下，有深溪环之 - When they reached the base of the mountain, there was a deep ravine surrounding it.
  • 乃编木以渡 - They tied wooden planks together to cross the ravine.
  • 绝岩翠竹之间，时见红彩，闻笑语声 - Between the sheer cliffs and green bamboo, they occasionally saw red colors and heard laughter and voices.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁大同未 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang (梁武帝) during the early Liang Dynasty (502–557 CE). '大同' is the era name of Emperor Wu, and '未' indicates the time before the end of this era.
  • 平南将军蔺钦 - 'General of the South, Lin Qin,' was a military officer during the Liang Dynasty. '平南将军' is a title for a general in charge of southern campaigns.
  • 李师古、陈彻 - These are the names of two leaders who were defeated by Lin Qin. They were likely local warlords or rebels during the Liang Dynasty.
  • 欧阳纥 - 'Ouyang He' was a general during the Liang Dynasty. He is the main character in this story.
  • 长乐 - A place name, likely a region or city that was conquered by Ouyang He.
  • 绣履 - 'Embroidered shoes' are a symbol of luxury and status in Chinese culture.

  • This phrase describes the interior of the stone gate, with wooden doors, spacious rooms like halls, walls with beds, and all covered with silk cushions.

  • This phrase describes the wife's reaction to seeing someone, with her glancing back with a single look and quickly waving her hand to send the person away.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遍身白毛，長數寸 - Describes a physical characteristic of having white fur all over the body, several inches long.
  • 所居常讀木簡，字若符篆，了不可識 - Refers to the creature's habit of reading wooden tablets with characters resembling ancient scripts, which are completely unreadable.
  • 已則置石磴下 - Indicates that after reading, the creature places the tablets under a stone step.
  • 晴昼或舞雙劍，環身電飛，光圓若月 - Describes the creature's behavior of dancing with two swords during sunny days, creating a circular light around its body like the moon.
  • 其飲食無常，喜啖果栗，尤嗜犬，咀而飲其血 - States that the creature's diet is unpredictable, but it enjoys eating fruits, chestnuts, and especially dogs, chewing and drinking their blood.
  • 日始逾午，即然而逝 - Means that after noon, the creature disappears.
  • 半昼往返數千里，及晚必歸 - Describes the creature's ability to travel thousands of miles in half a day and return by evening.
  • 夜就諸床嬲戲，一夕皆周，未嘗寢寐 - Indicates that at night, the creature plays with various beds, visiting each one in a single night without ever sleeping.
  • 言語淹詳，華旨會和 - Describes the creature's speech as eloquent and harmonious.
  • 然其狀，即暇狸之類也 - Concludes that despite these abilities, the creature resembles a kind of cat.
  • 吾為山神所訴，將得死罪 - The creature states that it has been accused by a mountain deity and faces death.
  • 亦求護之於眾靈，庶幾可免 - The creature seeks protection from various spirits, hoping to avoid punishment.
  • 前月哉生魄，石燈生火，焚其簡書 - Describes an event where a stone lamp ignites, burning the creature's tablets.
  • 吾已千歲，而無子。今有子，死期至矣 - The creature laments that after a thousand years without offspring, it now has a child, but its own death is imminent.
  • 因顧諸女，仇瀾者久之 - The creature looks at its daughters, lingering on one named Chou Lan.
  • 此山復絕，未嘗有人至。上高而望，絕不見樵者，下多虎狼怪獸 - Describes the isolation of the mountain, stating that it is so remote that no one ever visits, and from the top, one cannot see a single woodcutter, while the lower areas are inhabited by tigers, wolves, and other strange beasts.
  • 今能至者，非天假之何耶 - The creature wonders how anyone could reach such a place unless it were by divine intervention.
  • 及長，果文學善書，知名於時 - States that the creature's son grew up to be a scholar and calligrapher, becoming well-known in his time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少年欢醉后，必恐苦相留 - '少年欢醉' refers to youthful joy and intoxication; '必恐苦相留' means 'will surely fear to be painfully held back,' suggesting reluctance to be constrained.
  • 心常慕幽契，终不耻狂游 - '心常慕幽契' means 'the heart always longs for a deep, secret understanding'; '终不耻狂游' means 'will never be ashamed of wild wandering,' indicating a willingness to embrace a free and unconventional life.
  • 误入桃源里，仙家争肯留 - '误入桃源里' refers to mistakenly entering a utopian or idyllic place, alluding to the *Shi Shuo Xin Yu* story of the Peach Blossom Spring; '仙家争肯留' means 'the immortals will surely not keep me,' suggesting that even in an ideal place, the wanderer cannot stay.
  • 谁教他误入来？要不留，亦不得也 - '谁教他误入来' means 'Who made him mistakenly enter?'; '要不留，亦不得也' means 'If I want to keep him, I cannot,' implying a sense of inevitability.
  • 却恐不留，谁怕留千年万年 - '却恐不留' means 'yet fear not to keep him'; '谁怕留千年万年' means 'who would fear to keep him for thousands of years,' suggesting an enduring commitment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸳鸯伴 - A metaphor for a loyal and devoted partner, rooted in Chinese cultural symbolism.
  • 萍梗游 - A metaphor for a transient and unstable life, derived from classical poetry.
  • 桃源里 - Alludes to the *Shi Shuo Xin Yu* story of the Peach Blossom Spring, a utopian retreat.
  • 仙家 - Refers to immortals or spiritual beings in Chinese mythology.
  • 汉卓王孙家，文君慕相如 - Refers to the famous story of Zhuo Wenjun and Sima Xiangru from the Han dynasty, symbolizing love and devotion.

Proper Nouns

  • 卓王孙 - A historical figure from the Han dynasty, known for his daughter Zhuo Wenjun's relationship with Sima Xiangru.
  • 文君 - Another name for Zhuo Wenjun, a famous literary figure.
  • 相如 - Another name for Sima Xiangru, a renowned poet and musician.

  • 名与宦俱未称心 - Means 'neither fame nor official position has met my expectations.'
  • 沉迷于酒色 - Refers to being deeply engrossed in drinking and indulgence in sensual pleasures.
  • 月余不出 - Means 'for over a month without leaving.'
  • 非丈夫也 - A term of self-reproach, meaning 'not a true man.'
  • 妾是簪缨家女，君是宦途中人 - 'I am a daughter of a noble family, and you are a man on the path of officialdom.'
  • 与君匹偶，亦不相亏耳 - Means 'marrying you has not been a loss for me.'
  • 却欲以名宦荣身 - Refers to the desire to gain fame and prestige through official positions.
  • 思得诣金阕，谒明主也 - Means 'I wish to visit the imperial court to meet the wise ruler.'
  • 妾争敢固留君身，抑君显达乎 - A rhetorical question expressing the speaker's reluctance to hinder her husband's ambitions.
  • 何伤叹若是 - Means 'why should I lament like this?'
  • 幸夫人念我 - Means 'thankfully, my wife thinks of me.'
  • 元使我虚老蜀城 - Means 'she has saved me from wasting my life in Sichuan.'
  • 可珍重藏之 - Means 'you should treasure and keep it carefully.'
  • 我阿母与我幼时所弄之物也 - Means 'it is something my mother and I played with when I was young.'
  • 乃吟诗一首以送 - Means 'she then composed a poem to send him off.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 诗书 - Refers to the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*) and the *Book of Documents* (*Shujing*), two of the Five Classics.
  • 名宦 - Refers to prestigious official positions in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 簪缨家女 - Refers to a woman from a noble or high-ranking family, indicated by the ornamental hairpins (*zānyīng*) worn by such families.
  • 宦途中人 - Refers to someone on the path of officialdom, pursuing a career in government.
  • 金阕 - Refers to the imperial palace, a symbol of the emperor's authority.
  • 明主 - Refers to a wise and just ruler.
  • 蜀城 - Refers to Chengdu, a city in Sichuan province, historically known for its cultural and political significance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于是坐于西阶上 - Formal expression indicating the speaker's position on the western step.
  • 移其马于堂北 - Means 'moved his horse to the north of the hall,' setting the scene.
  • 令一仆侍立于前，若为傧而待之 - Means 'ordered a servant to stand in front, as if serving as an usher,' describing the arrangement of the servant.
  • 未几，火光照耀，军马骄阗 - Describes a scene of bright firelight and the presence of military horses, indicating a grand or military context.
  • 二紫衣吏入而复走出曰：“相公在此。” - Formal announcement by two officials dressed in purple, indicating their high status.
  • 逡巡一黄衣吏入而出，亦曰：“相公在此。” - Another formal announcement by an official dressed in yellow, reinforcing the presence of a high-ranking individual.
  • 公私心独喜：“吾当为宰相，必胜此鬼矣。” - Expresses the speaker's private joy and confidence in his future role.
  • 既而，将军渐下，导吏复告之 - Indicates the arrival of a general, with officials guiding him.
  • 将军曰：“人。”有戈剑弓矢，翼引以人，即东阶下 - Describes the general's arrival and the accompanying armed guards.
  • 公使仆前曰：“郭秀才见。”遂行揖 - The speaker sends a servant to announce his presence and performs a bow.
  • 将军者，喜而延坐，与对食，言笑极欢 - Describes the general's warm reception and the ensuing friendly interaction.

  • 乌将军，此乡镇神 - Literally 'General Wu, the town's guardian spirit,' meaning the god of the town.
  • 乡人奉之久矣 - Literally 'the villagers have worshipped him for a long time,' meaning the god has been worshipped by the villagers for a long time.
  • 岁配以女 - Literally 'annually offering a girl to him,' meaning the annual ritual of offering a girl.
  • 才无他虞 - Literally 'only then is there no other danger,' meaning only then is there no other problem.

  • 即风雨雷雹为虐 - Literally 'then wind, rain, thunder, and hail will cause harm,' meaning natural disasters will occur.
  • 奈何失路之客 - Literally 'how could a traveler who has lost his way,' meaning how could a traveler who has gone astray.
  • 而伤我明神 - Literally 'and injure our bright god,' meaning and harm our god.
  • 致暴于人 - Literally 'cause violence against people,' meaning cause harm to people.

  • 将令执公 - Literally 'to order them to arrest the gentleman,' meaning they planned to arrest him.
  • 谕之曰 - Literally 'informed them, saying,' meaning he explained to them.
  • 尔徒老于年 - Literally 'you are old in years,' meaning you are old in age.
  • 未老于事 - Literally 'but not old in matters,' meaning but not wise in matters.
  • 我天下之达理者 - Literally 'I am one who understands the ways of the world,' meaning I am someone who understands the principles of the world.
  • 尔众听吾言 - Literally 'you all listen to my words,' meaning you all listen to what I say.
  • 夫神，受天之命 - Literally 'as for gods, they receive the mandate of heaven,' meaning gods are appointed by heaven.
  • 而为镇也 - Literally 'and serve as guardians,' meaning and act as protectors.
  • 不若诸侯，受命于天子 - Literally 'unlike feudal lords, who receive their mandate from the emperor,' meaning unlike feudal lords, who are appointed by the emperor.
  • 而疆理天下乎 - Literally 'and govern the world,' meaning and rule the world.
  • 然 - Literally 'yes,' meaning yes.
  • 使诸侯渔色于国中 - Literally 'if feudal lords seek pleasure within their states,' meaning if feudal lords indulge in pleasure within their states.
  • 天子不怒乎 - Literally 'would the emperor not be angry,' meaning would the emperor not be angry.
  • 残虐于人 - Literally 'if they are cruel to people,' meaning if they are cruel to people.
  • 天子不伐乎 - Literally 'would the emperor not punish them,' meaning would the emperor not punish them.
  • 诚使尔呼将军者，真神明也 - Literally 'if indeed the one you call General Wu is truly a god,' meaning if indeed the god you call General Wu is truly a god.
  • 神固无猪蹄 - Literally 'gods would not have pig's feet,' meaning gods would not have pig's feet.
  • 天岂使淫妖之兽乎 - Literally 'would heaven make a perverse and monstrous beast,' meaning would heaven make a perverse and monstrous beast.
  • 且妖淫之兽 - Literally 'and a perverse and monstrous beast,' meaning and a perverse and monstrous beast.
  • 天地之罪畜也 - Literally 'is a sinful creature of heaven and earth,' meaning is a sinful creature of heaven and earth.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天方曙 - Refers to the early morning, a time often associated with the beginning of a new day or a new chapter in classical Chinese literature.
  • 乌将军 - Refers to General Wu, a deity or spirit worshipped by the villagers, often associated with protection and guardianship.
  • 乡镇神 - Refers to the god of the town, a local deity worshipped by the villagers.
  • 岁配以女 - Refers to the annual ritual of offering a girl to the god, a practice sometimes found in ancient Chinese religious customs.
  • 风雨雷雹为虐 - Refers to natural disasters, which were often seen as divine punishment in classical Chinese thought.
  • 失路之客 - Refers to a traveler who has gone astray, a metaphor often used in classical Chinese literature to describe someone who has strayed from the right path.
  • 明神 - Refers to a bright or enlightened god, often associated with protection and benevolence.
  • 诸侯 - Refers to feudal lords, rulers of smaller states within the empire, who were appointed by the emperor.
  • 天子 - Refers to the emperor, the supreme ruler of the empire, who was seen as the son of heaven and the ultimate authority.
  • 淫妖之兽 - Refers to a perverse and monstrous beast, often associated with chaos and disorder in classical Chinese thought.
